title: Lock-step multi-product sweep
summary: Move multiple products together through parallel value lists, producing one simulation point per position.
description: Runs the choice simulator for each position in a set of parallel value lists, applying the i-th value from each product's attribute list simultaneously. Unlike sim_sensitivities (one product/attribute at a time) or sim_combinations expand_grid (cartesian product), lock_step moves all products in unison: at position 0 ProductA gets price[0] and ProductB gets price[0]; at position 1 ProductA gets price[1] and ProductB gets price[1]; and so on. This is the natural model for industry-wide price movements, inflation scenarios, or portfolio repricing.

arguments.name: product_names
arguments.description: Subset of product names from scenario_chgs to include in the lock-step sweep. When omitted, all products in scenario_chgs participate. Use this to restrict which products move together when scenario_chgs contains more products than you want in the sweep.

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures. Any subset of: share, revenue, volume, cost, profit, standard_error. Use "__all__" for all six.

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario in the sim_db.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Subgroup column names. Use ["__all__"] for every subgroup column.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names. Use ["__all__"] for all weights.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows summed by product_net group.

arguments.name: exponents
arguments.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s). Single method or a pipe-separated blend (e.g. "sop|top2"). Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit where products are grouped by scenario attribute <att>, with <bot_spec> within groups and <top_spec> across groups (e.g. "sop:sop-Brand", "top1:sop-Category"); "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop implicitly. Blend methods with | and weights via prob_methods_wgts.

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: price_formulas
arguments.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived prices, applied before each lock-step point.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Parallel value lists keyed by product_name. All lists should be the same length; the sweep stops at the shortest. Example: {ProductA: {price: [9, 10, 11]}, ProductB: {price: [14, 15, 16]}}.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the points × scenarios × weights fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Each lock-step position is scored independently. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

details:
The implementation uses zip semantics: all attribute value lists across all participating products are zipped together positionally. Position 0 of every list is applied simultaneously in the first simulation, position 1 in the second, and so on.

If product_names is supplied, only the listed products are included in the zip; other products listed in scenario_chgs are ignored. This allows a single scenario_chgs block to serve both sim_lock_step (filtered to a subset) and sim_run (with the full override).

Scenario labels in the output encode the position: "base|ProductA|price|9.00|ProductB|price|14.00".

All value lists should be the same length for predictable results; the zip stops at the shortest list. Scalar values (single numbers) are treated as one-element lists.
value: Writes sim_lock_step_0.csv. Scenario column encodes the position, e.g., "base|ProductA|price|9.00|ProductB|price|14.00". In protocol mode emits event.run.started, event.artifact.created, event.run.finished.
notes: All value lists should be the same length; the sweep stops at the shortest list.
Use sim_combinations with expand_grid = true if you want the full cartesian product instead of parallel movement.
product_names is a filter on the keys of scenario_chgs, not on the products in the sim_db.

see_also.description: Cartesian product or zip sweep (expand_grid = false is identical to lock_step without product_names filtering).
One product/attribute at a time sensitivity sweep.

examples.title: Industry-wide price movement
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_lock_step"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = [9.0, 10.0, 11.0]

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductB]
price = [14.0, 15.0, 16.0]

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductC]
price = [12.0, 13.0, 14.0]

examples.title: Filtered subset of products
examples.body:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_lock_step"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|revenue"
product_names = ["ProductA", "ProductB"]

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductA]
price = [9.0, 10.0, 11.0]

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductB]
price = [14.0, 15.0, 16.0]

["cs::sim_lock_step".scenario_chgs.ProductC]
price = [5.0, 6.0, 7.0]

fields.name: product_names
fields.description: Product names to include. Omit to include all products in scenario_chgs.

fields.name: measures
fields.description: Pipe-separated measures. Default: share|revenue.

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: weights
fields.description: Weight column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows summed by product_net group. Default: false.

fields.name: exponents
fields.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: prob_methods_wgts
fields.description: Blend weights for prob_methods. Default: [1.0].

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: trip_seeds
fields.description: Random seed for trip simulation. Default: 100.

fields.name: price_formulas
fields.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived prices.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: scenario_chgs
fields.description: Parallel value lists: {product: {attr: [v1, v2, ...]}}.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for points × scenarios × weights fan-out. Default: 1.

artifacts.title: Lock-step sweep results
artifacts.description: Simulation results from sweeping multiple products through parallel value lists simultaneously. Models industry-wide price movements or portfolio repricing scenarios. One row per position × scenario × subgroup × product.

fields.name: scenario
fields.description: Position label encoding the attribute values applied, e.g. 'base|ProductA|price|9.00|ProductB|price|14.00'.

fields.name: subgroup
fields.description: Respondent subgroup.

fields.name: product
fields.description: Product being measured.

fields.name: measure
fields.description: Metric type.

fields.name: value
fields.description: Simulated value.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Standard deviation across posterior draws.
